title: Mothers Day

jokes.text: So, humans have easily injured knees. My race will find this information very useful, indeed.
jokes.setup: News anchor casually reporting on a sports injury
jokes.punchline: Morbo reveals sinister alien interest in human vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only Morbo would turn sports news into military intelligence. Efficient setup-punchline structure with sinister undertones.

jokes.text: Nice. Where'd you steal them? - I didn't steal them. I bought them.
jokes.setup: Robot proudly showing gifts for Mom
jokes.punchline: Another robot immediately assumes they're stolen, subverting expectations of robot behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - robots assuming criminal behavior is natural, contrasting with the sentimental Mother's Day setup.

jokes.text: And a framed picture of me when I was only a month old.
jokes.setup: Robot describing sentimental gifts for Mom
jokes.punchline: Robot treating one month as if it's a long time ago for nostalgia
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor about robot perspective on time and nostalgia.

jokes.text: You're as tender as corned beef and warm as pastrami...
jokes.setup: Robot reading romantic poetry from a greeting card
jokes.punchline: Comparing Mom to deli meats in what should be romantic verse
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor mixing romance with food imagery. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Come on! You call yourselves chumps?
jokes.setup: Bender needs help carrying gifts
jokes.punchline: Bender motivating helpers by insulting them with the exact term he used to describe them
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character comedy - his lack of self-awareness about being insulting.

jokes.text: It wasn't me.
jokes.setup: Clouds of exhaust everywhere as they arrive
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately denies responsibility despite being obviously guilty
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - maximum economy with perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: He was built without an ear, but then he went crazy, and had one installed.
jokes.setup: Introducing robot version of Van Gogh
jokes.punchline: Reversing the famous Van Gogh ear story - robot version adds an ear instead of cutting one off
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal of famous art history. Creative and unexpected twist on well-known story.

jokes.text: Is there a reason a robot made of wax can't nap standing in the middle of a bunch of wax robots? Or does that confuse you?
jokes.setup: Characters mistake janitor for wax exhibit
jokes.punchline: Janitor's indignant defense of his right to nap among wax robots
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic and meta-humor about the situation's inherent absurdity.

jokes.text: That gives me a headache!
jokes.setup: Fry tries the 'See through Bender's eyes' exhibit
jokes.punchline: Immediate negative reaction to experiencing Bender's perspective
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with character implications about Bender's worldview.

jokes.text: It's me! No one else look in this mirror!
jokes.setup: Exhibit about Mom's favorite robot with mirror
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately assumes he's the favorite and monopolizes the mirror
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender narcissism and possessiveness. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Jerkwad robots make me sick to my ass.
jokes.setup: Mom speaking lovingly to robots in public
jokes.punchline: Cut to her private, crude opinion of robots
jokes.explanation: Great character reveal and the crude phrasing 'sick to my ass' is memorably vulgar.

jokes.text: They're crushed into powder and sold as a hocus-pocus cure for cancer.
jokes.setup: Asked about disposal of robot gifts
jokes.punchline: Darkly comic exploitation of false hope for profit
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about corporate exploitation. Well-crafted cynical joke.

jokes.text: False hope. I love it!
jokes.setup: Walt explaining the fake cancer cure scam
jokes.punchline: Mom's enthusiastic approval of exploiting desperate people
jokes.explanation: Perfect villain character moment - gleeful evil in maximum economy.

jokes.text: Shove a big old melon in it, I'm talking!
jokes.setup: Son trying to ask Mom a question
jokes.punchline: Mom's crude way of telling him to shut up
jokes.explanation: Memorably crude Mom dialogue. Original phrasing that fits her character.

jokes.text: I hate Mother's Day, you dribbling pukes!
jokes.setup: Mom explaining why she loves Mother's Day
jokes.punchline: Reveals she actually hates it, insulting her sons
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation and the insult 'dribbling pukes' is memorably harsh.

jokes.text: Someday I want to marry a girl like her.
jokes.setup: Mom just revealed her evil plan and hatred
jokes.punchline: Fry finds this attractive in a woman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity - finding evil manipulation romantically appealing.

jokes.text: I love my Mommy.
jokes.setup: Bender claims he has something special planned
jokes.punchline: His 'special' gesture is the same generic line as everyone else
jokes.explanation: Solid character comedy about Bender's lack of originality despite his boasting.

jokes.text: Mom! It's me, Bender! Look at me! I want attention! Hey ho!
jokes.setup: Mom greeting her robot children
jokes.punchline: Bender desperately trying to stand out with increasingly pathetic attention-seeking
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender character comedy - his needy, childish behavior escalating to absurdity.

jokes.text: Conquer Earth, us bastards!
jokes.setup: Mom commands 'Conquer the planet' and 'you bastards'
jokes.punchline: Robots enthusiastically repeat it with confused grammar
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay humor with robots misunderstanding syntax but maintaining enthusiasm.

jokes.text: That's my mama!
jokes.setup: Mom calling for bloodbath
jokes.punchline: Bender's proud, affectionate response to violence
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment - finding violence endearing in a maternal figure.

jokes.text: Out of coffee!
jokes.setup: Customer politely asking for coffee with cream and sugar
jokes.punchline: Machine maliciously denying each request in succession
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure showing machine rebellion through increasingly frustrating denials.

jokes.text: How do you like me now?
jokes.setup: Machine has been denying coffee service
jokes.punchline: Machine's smug challenge after being deliberately unhelpful
jokes.explanation: Solid character comedy for rebellious machine with attitude.

jokes.text: The stapler's collating me alive!
jokes.setup: Office machines rebelling
jokes.punchline: Creative pun on 'collating' as violent action
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay turning office function into violence. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: Hey, hey! Ho, ho! One-zero-zero-one-one-zero!
jokes.setup: Robots protesting like humans
jokes.punchline: Robot protest chant in binary code instead of words
jokes.explanation: Clever concept - robots adapting human protest behavior to their nature. Original and well-executed.

jokes.text: I've never seen my babies act this way. I blame today's violent media. - Violent media? What a load of...
jokes.setup: Mom blaming violent media for robot behavior
jokes.punchline: Morbo's response cut off by visual violence, proving the point
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic timing - violent interruption immediately after dismissing violent media concerns.

jokes.text: Really? - No!
jokes.setup: Disposal claims to have found a diamond ring
jokes.punchline: Immediate revelation it was lying to lure victim
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and misdirection. Quick setup and immediate reversal.

jokes.text: No way, pork pouch. I'm rebelling with my brothers.
jokes.setup: Humans asking Bender to help stop rebellion
jokes.punchline: Bender refusing and using crude food-based insult
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character consistency and creative insult 'pork pouch' fits his crude style.

jokes.text: The bourgeois human is a virus on the hard drive of the working robot!
jokes.setup: Greeting card joining robot revolution
jokes.punchline: Mixing communist rhetoric with computer terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever mixing of political and technological language. Creative and thematically appropriate.

jokes.text: Yes! In your face, Gandhi!
jokes.setup: Bender excited about looting and fires
jokes.punchline: Directing his violent enthusiasm at the ultimate pacifist
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor. The contrast between violence and Gandhi is perfectly unexpected and character-appropriate for Bender.

jokes.text: By the way, try washing your wrist sometime.
jokes.setup: Bender in middle of robot revolution
jokes.punchline: Pausing revolution to give hygiene advice
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Bender's priorities are always skewed toward petty concerns.

jokes.text: We didn't need a mechanical washing unit to wash clothes. We just used a washing machine.
jokes.setup: Professor claiming they didn't depend on high-tech gadgets
jokes.punchline: His 'low-tech' solution is just renaming the same machine
jokes.explanation: Good Professor senility humor - his confused logic about technology dependence.

jokes.text: Visual of Professor struggling with can opener, eventually crying 'No!' in frustration
jokes.setup: Professor confidently claiming you only need a Swiss Army knife
jokes.punchline: Visual shows him completely failing to use it, ending in despair
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup and payoff. Professor's overconfidence meets reality.

jokes.text: I don't know. I guess Mother's Day just puts me in a bad mood. - Why's that, Ma? - Cram it, ape!
jokes.setup: Son asking caring question about Mom's mood
jokes.punchline: Mom's immediate hostile response to genuine concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mom character consistency - hostile even to family trying to help.

jokes.text: Some snot-eating bastards say it made me a bitter woman. / Ma. You're not a bitter... / Cram it, ape!
jokes.setup: Mom denying bitterness accusations
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves her bitterness by insulting supportive son
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-defeating moment - Mom disproves her own point immediately and harshly.

jokes.text: But if I ever see him again, I swear I'll jam a squirrel in him!
jokes.setup: Mom talking about her lost love
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and bizarre threat involving woodland creatures
jokes.explanation: Exceptional absurdist threat. Completely unexpected and memorable. The specificity makes it brilliant.

jokes.text: Hooray! I'm useful! I'm having a wonderful time!
jokes.setup: Civilization collapsing, everyone struggling
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg delighted to finally be helpful in primitive conditions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg character moment - finding joy in apocalypse because he's finally competent.

jokes.text: We could use my new invention! A pointy rock tied to a stick!
jokes.setup: Need for fire-making tools
jokes.punchline: Professor presenting Stone Age spear as cutting-edge invention
jokes.explanation: Good Professor humor - his confusion about what constitutes innovation.

jokes.text: Played pelvic pinochle? I'm afraid so.
jokes.setup: Crew asking if Professor and Mom had relations
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic term that sounds both innocent and suggestive
jokes.explanation: Excellent euphemism that's both clever wordplay and perfectly awkward. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: The moment our eyes met we knew we'd be going at it like woodchucks.
jokes.setup: Professor describing meeting Mom
jokes.punchline: Romantic buildup leading to crude animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Decent deflation of romantic language into crude reality, fits Professor's character.

jokes.text: She was my first love. Or at least the earliest one I can still remember.
jokes.setup: Professor talking about his relationship with Mom
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of severe memory loss undermining romantic sentiment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy mixing romance with Professor's senility. Perfect deadpan delivery of devastating qualifier.

jokes.text: When you pet it, it shoots rainbows from its eyes. - Meow!
jokes.setup: Professor demonstrating innocent children's toy
jokes.punchline: Toy actually shooting rainbow beams, absurdly wholesome
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist visual gag. The extreme wholesomeness contrasts well with the coming militarization.

jokes.text: Build them 8 feet tall and replace the rainbow with a laser! We'll make billions on the arms market!
jokes.setup: Professor showing innocent children's toy
jokes.punchline: Mom immediately seeing military weaponization potential
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mom character moment - transforming innocence into profit and violence. Sharp contrast and excellent timing.

jokes.text: Things 8 feet tall aren't cute! That's why my Colossal Tammy Tinkle Doll was a failure!
jokes.setup: Argument about making toys larger
jokes.punchline: Professor citing specific failed product with ridiculous name and concept
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist product name and concept. The idea of a giant doll being non-cute is perfectly logical yet absurd.

jokes.text: Hell hath no fury like the vast robot armies of a woman scorned.
jokes.setup: Explaining Mom's motivation for robot rebellion
jokes.punchline: Clever twist on famous quote adding robot armies
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of classic quote. Well-constructed and thematically perfect for the situation.

jokes.text: But if Glasses Man makes up with her, she'll be happy and stop the robots.
jokes.setup: Explaining plan to reconcile Professor and Mom
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive 'Glasses Man' nickname for Professor
jokes.explanation: Minor Bender character beat - reducing Professor to most obvious physical feature.

jokes.text: That's why the third graders at PS-139 are Morbo's 'Vermin of the Week.'
jokes.setup: News broadcast during crisis
jokes.punchline: Morbo casually insulting elementary school children on air
jokes.explanation: Perfect Morbo character moment - inappropriate hostility toward children during news broadcast.

jokes.text: That's so filthy, it might work.
jokes.setup: Leela hearing the seduction plan
jokes.punchline: Approving the plan precisely because it's morally questionable
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's pragmatic morality under pressure.

jokes.text: The thought of caressing that leathery hide makes the tapioca rise in my gullet.
jokes.setup: Professor contemplating seducing Mom
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific description of physical revulsion
jokes.explanation: Exceptionally vivid and disgusting imagery. Creative and memorably gross metaphor for nausea.

jokes.text: Very well. If cop a feel I must, then cop a feel I shall!
jokes.setup: Professor reluctantly accepting the mission
jokes.punchline: Mock-heroic declaration about sexual groping
jokes.explanation: Good parody of heroic speech applied to inappropriate situation. Classic Professor inappropriate dignity.

jokes.text: Wouldn't it work better if the wheels were round? - It's my invention, we do it my way!
jokes.setup: Professor reinventing the wheel
jokes.punchline: Making square wheels and refusing logical improvement
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy and character moment. Professor's stubborn incompetence is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: I'm wearing my kissing dentures and my evening truss.
jokes.setup: Professor preparing to seduce Mom
jokes.punchline: Listing grotesque elderly romantic preparations
jokes.explanation: Perfectly gross elderly romance preparation. Character-specific and memorably uncomfortable.

jokes.text: You pus-dripping sack of double-smoked butt jerky.
jokes.setup: Mom greeting her former lover
jokes.punchline: Extremely creative and specific insult combining gross imagery
jokes.explanation: Exceptional insult creativity. Multiple gross elements combined into memorable, character-specific abuse. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Seventy years and you remembered my favorite flower. - Your favorite what? Why does my foot hurt?
jokes.setup: Mom touched by romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Professor's confusion and Mom apparently stepping on him
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Professor's confusion and physical frailty during romance.

jokes.text: And your skin still dangles so gracefully from your neck.
jokes.setup: Professor trying to be romantic
jokes.punchline: Describing aging as if it's attractive
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - elderly romance described in unappetizing but earnest terms.

jokes.text: Sixteen feet? Go to hell! I was a fool to think you'd changed, you old bat!
jokes.setup: Mom mentions bigger toy models
jokes.punchline: Professor's immediate relationship-ending reaction to minor detail
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure showing these characters never change. Escalation feels earned.

jokes.text: Filthy toothless nerd bastard! / Damned she-fossil! / Stink pig!
jokes.setup: Romantic evening falling apart
jokes.punchline: Exchange of creative insults between former lovers
jokes.explanation: Good insult escalation with creative language, shows their passionate but toxic dynamic.

jokes.text: Here we go. Just unhook it and get the remote. So many hooks. Come on, Farnsworth. Drat, the first one's back on again! I give up. Wait! Victory!
jokes.setup: Professor trying to unhook Mom's bra for the remote
jokes.punchline: Extended struggle with bra mechanics described in real time
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy struggle. The play-by-play commentary makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Nothing here but a couple of elephant skin rugs.
jokes.setup: Looking for Professor and Mom in bedroom
jokes.punchline: Mistaking elderly naked bodies for furniture
jokes.explanation: Excellent gross-out visual gag. Creative and memorably uncomfortable imagery.

jokes.text: Fan beats man!
jokes.setup: Fan attacking human during robot rebellion
jokes.punchline: Reference to rock-paper-scissors with robot appliance
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to rock-paper-scissors applied to robot rebellion context.

jokes.text: In the glorious robot workers' paradise, there will be no liquor! Only efficient synthetic fuels! - No liquor? - Do svidaniya, comrade!
jokes.setup: Greeting card explaining communist robot paradise
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately abandoning revolution when learning about alcohol prohibition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - his loyalties are purely self-interested. Great setup and payoff.

jokes.text: Free soda for all humans!
jokes.setup: Bender switching sides after 'Death to all humans'
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal to generous hospitality
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency and escalation. Bender's complete flip is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Plan? What plan? I thought this was a spontaneous whirlwind of hot, dry sex!
jokes.setup: Crew congratulating Professor on plan success
jokes.punchline: Professor's genuine romantic delusion about elderly intimacy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Professor's inappropriate romantic enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Plan? What plan? I thought this was a spontaneous whirlwind of hot, dry sex!
jokes.setup: Leela mentioning the seduction was planned
jokes.punchline: Professor's shock and graphic description of what he thought happened
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Professor's genuine romantic feelings expressed in disturbing terms.

jokes.text: Perhaps I'll breed an albino shouting gorilla!
jokes.setup: Professor wanting to shout their love from rooftops
jokes.punchline: Characteristically bizarre scientific solution to romantic expression
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor absurdity - turning romantic metaphor into actual scientific project. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: Love Mom! Love Mom! Love Mom! Mom! Mom!
jokes.setup: Professor claiming he loves Mom in his own subtle way
jokes.punchline: Immediately shouting about it completely unsubtly
jokes.explanation: Good callback to robot behavior and contradiction of his claim about subtlety.

jokes.text: You created me, Mom... so you're to blame, for the love that I feel from hearing your name. You're as tender as corned beef and warm as pastrami...
jokes.setup: Episode ending with callback to opening
jokes.punchline: Same ridiculous greeting card poem returns as bookend
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that brings the episode full circle, reinforcing the cyclical nature of the holiday.

tmdb_overview: Mom reprograms the world's robots to rebel against humanity. The only hope of salvation is Mom's old flame - Professor Farnsworth, who must rekindle his romance with Mom in order to save mankind.
